category: Buffer Access with Incorrect Length Value**
description: Incorrect storage packing in Solidity occurs when storage variables are not efficiently packed within a single storage slot, leading to unnecessary gas consumption. Solidity automatically stores variables in 32-byte (256-bit) slots, and inefficient ordering of data types can lead to wasted space. Contracts that fail to optimize storage packing may incur higher gas costs during deployments and transactions.
name: Incorrect Storage Packing
remediation: To optimize storage packing: - Group smaller data types together (e.g., `uint8`, `bool`) to fit within a single 32-byte slot. - Avoid leaving gaps between variables of different sizes. - Order state variables efficiently to minimize wasted storage slots.
